description_long: This is a tall, slender ceramic vessel with a narrow neck, a single curved handle, and a flared mouth. The body is predominantly white with intricate black and brown decorative motifs, including geometric patterns and stylized floral or shell-like designs. The neck and base are accented with black, while the handle is robust and attached from the rim to the shoulder. The vessel has a classic amphora or lekythos shape, reminiscent of ancient Greek pottery, and appears to be hand-painted. The surface is matte with visible brushwork and fine detailing. There are no visible labels, branding, or modern markings on the exterior.
description: A tall, slender ceramic bottle with a single handle, flared mouth, and intricate black and brown decorative motifs on a white body. The vessel has a matte finish and classic ancient Greek styling.
description_view_0: This view highlights the handle and the flared mouth, with minimal decoration visible on the body.
description_view_1: This view shows the decorative motifs on the body, including geometric and shell-like patterns, and the handle is visible to the left.
description_view_2: This view provides a clear look at the main decorative motifs and the transition between the neck and the body.
description_view_3: This view focuses on the back of the vessel, showing the handle's attachment and less of the decorative motifs.
